findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Password
findings.description: The product contains a hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication or for outbound communication to external components.

findings.name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
findings.description: The product allows the upload or transfer of dangerous file types that are automatically processed within its environment.
findings.evidence: src/main/java/com/photoalbum/service/impl/PhotoServiceImpl.java:83 - File type validation based only on MIME type from MultipartFile.getContentType(), but no validation of file content (magic bytes). Uploader can bypass MIME type checks by changing Content-Type header.

findings.name: Incorrect Conversion between Numeric Types
findings.description: When converting from one data type to another, such as long to integer, data can be omitted or translated in a way that produces unexpected values. If the resulting values are used in a sensitive context, then dangerous behaviors may occur.
findings.evidence: src/main/java/com/photoalbum/service/impl/PhotoServiceImpl.java:125-126 - image.getWidth() and image.getHeight() return int values assigned to Integer wrapper type, with potential implicit conversion from long (file.getSize()) to Long wrapper used in Photo entity

findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
findings.description: The product contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key.
findings.evidence: src/main/resources/application.properties:11-12 - spring.datasource.username=photoalbum and spring.datasource.password=photoalbum; src/main/resources/application-docker.properties:3-4 - spring.datasource.username=photoalbum and spring.datasource.password=photoalbum
